name: US.PCI
Title: Productive capacities index, annual, 2000-2024 (analytical)
Keywords: Productive capacities index, PCI, structural transformation, natural capital, human capital, energy, transport, ICT, institutions, structural change, private sector
Author: UNCTAD
Notes:
Description:

The PCI was developed as an official metric to measure and benchmark productive capacities, following requests from member States through the Nairobi Maafikiano (TD/519/Add.2, para. 76(k)) and ECOSOC (E/RES/2017/29, para. 6) resolutions. It builds on UNCTAD’s conceptual framework for measuring productive capacities, structured around three pillars: “productive resources, entrepreneurial capabilities, and production linkages, which together determine a country’s capacity to produce goods and services and enable it to grow and develop” (UNCTAD, 2006, 2025a).

The PCI is further disaggregated into eight categories: natural capital, human capital, energy, institutions, private sector, structural change, transport, and information and communication technologies. These categories translate the three overarching pillars into actionable policy domains and reflect UNCTAD’s research on the optimal configuration of productive capacity dimensions for policy relevance.

The Table presents nine PCI scores: one for each category and an overall score that aggregates the eight categories into a single composite indicator.

Sources:

UNCTAD calculations based on 43 input indicators from international sources. The use of international sources ensures coherence and comparability across countries. The data sources are the following:

  • World Bank, Sustainable Energy for All (SE4ALL)
  • International Energy Agency, World Energy Balances
  • UN Development Program
  • UNESCO Institute for Statistics
  • Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation, Global Health Data Exchange
  • World Health Organization, Global Health Expenditure database
  • United Nations Population Division. World Population Prospects: 2024 Revision.
  • Census reports and other statistical publications from national statistical offices
  • Eurostat: Demographic Statistics
  • United Nations Statistical Division. Population and Vital Statistics Report (various years)
  • United States Census Bureau: International Database
  • Secretariat of the Pacific Community: Statistics and Demography Programme
  • International Telecommunication Union (ITU), World Telecommunication/ICT Development Report and database
  • World Bank, World Development Indicators
  • World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO)
  • World Bank, Worldwide Governance Indicators
  • Food and Agriculture Organization
  • Materialflows.net
  • United Nations Statistics Division, National Accounts - Analysis of Main Aggregates (AMA)
  • SDG Global Database
  • International Monetary Fund, International Financial Statistics
  • International Civil Aviation Organization
  • International Road Federation, World Road Statistics 2024 Millennium Dataset (www.irfofficial.org / www.worldroadstatistics.org)
  • International Union of Railways, International Railway Statistics
  • Other tables in UNCTADstat

Notes:

The PCI is calculated as a geometric average of the indices for the eight categories. Each category index is obtained from the principal components extracted from the underlying indicators, weighted by their capacity to explain the variance of the original data. The category indices are normalized into 1-100 intervals.

Cells where the score where either the majority or all of the underlying indicators have been filled through interpolation, forecasting or imputation are flagged. The imputation step involves a stochastic process that in some cases give different results from one run of the process to the other. Where the consequence of this stochasticity is significant the same cell notes indicate this as well. The cell notes identify cases where the range of the published value, due to randomness in the process, vary more than ±1 index point and when the value varies more than ±3 point.  This range corresponds to the range of 95% of results of 200 identical runs of the same analysis only varying in the random seed.

The table uses the following cell notes:

Imputation rate

Range of 95% of repeated runs

Note

< 50%

(No note)

Run-to-run variation exceeds ±1 index point

>= 50%

Half or more of the indicators imputed. Value stable across runs (variation within ± 1 index point).

>= 50%

Half or more of the indicators imputed. Run-to-run variation exceeds ±1 index points.

>= 50%

Half or more of the indicators imputed. Run-to-run variation exceeds +/- 3 index points.

100%

<= 2

All indicators imputed. Value stable across runs (variation within ±1 index point).

100%

> 2

All indicators imputed. Run-to-run variation exceeds ±1 index points.

100%

> 6

All indicators imputed. Run-to-run variation exceeds ±3 index points.


Values for groups of economies are the medians of group member values.

A detailed description of the 3rd generation methodology  is presented in UNCTAD (2025b). The main methodological change from the previous, 2nd generation  PCI update (UNCTAD, 2023) is the inclusion of an input indicator on maritime transport within the Transport category.

The PCI is overseen by the Statistical and Technical Advisory Group (STAG) on the Productive Capacities Index, a peer-review body of experts that ensures the methodological integrity of the PCI, aligns its indicators with international standards, and evaluates the inclusion of new dimensions and data sources. A summary report of the peer-review process for this update is also available online (UNCTAD, 2025c).

 

References:

UNCTAD (2006). The Least Developed Countries Report 2006: Developing Productive Capacities. United Nations publication. Sales No. E.06.II.D.9. New York and Geneva.

UNCTAD (2023). Productive Capacities Index: 2nd Generation  - Enhanced Statistical and Methodological Approach with Results (UNCTAD/ALDC/2023/2). Available at https://unctad.org/publication/productive-capacities-index-2nd-generation (accessed 28 March 2024).

UNCTAD (2025a). Statistical Guidelines for Measuring Productive Capacities. Forthcoming.

UNCTAD (2025b). Productive Capacities Index: 3rd Generation - Enhanced Statistical and Methodological Approach with Results. Forthcoming.
